disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden appearance over ~12 hours of large tense fluid-filled blisters on both lower legs, starting as small red patches
trigger_question_examples: When did the blisters start and how did they change?
Where on the body are they?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They came up today over about half a day. They began as little red patches on both shins and then blew up into big taut blisters full of clear fluid.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The blister fluid is clear (not cloudy, not yellow, no pus)
trigger_question_examples: What does the fluid inside the blisters look like?
Is any of it cloudy or yellow?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The fluid inside is clear, like water — none of it is cloudy or yellow.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Only mild skin tightness — not severely painful, minimal itch, no radiating heat felt
trigger_question_examples: Does the skin around them feel hot to you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It just feels a bit tight. It is not really sore and it does not itch much, and I have not noticed heat coming off it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no chills, feels generally well — no systemic illness reported
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, chills or shakes?
Do you feel unwell in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or chills at all. In myself I feel fine — it is just the blisters that worry the nurses.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new medications started recently (facility chart checked — no new antibiotics or diuretics)
trigger_question_examples: Have any new medicines been started in the last few weeks?
Any new antibiotics or water tablets recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The nurses checked my chart — nothing new has been started, no new antibiotics or water tablets.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Frail, mobility-impaired aged-care resident who uses a walker; nursing staff can do daily monitoring
trigger_question_examples: How do you normally get around?
Who helps look after you day to day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I use a walker and I am a bit frail. The nursing staff here look after me and can keep an eye on things.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The surrounding skin is only mildly pink, without spreading redness up the leg or a hard swollen feel (as observed/described)
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness spreading up the leg?
Does the area feel hard or swollen compared with the other leg?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The skin around them is just a little pink. It does not seem to be spreading up my leg and it does not feel hard.
